items.title: Docker
items.description: A platform for containerizing applications. Docker is definitely my MVP of all tools; I'm not only comfortable using containerization in DevOps and for operating my home server, but I also just really enjoy working with Docker and Compose.

items.title: Ansible
items.description: Infrastructure as Code tool. I love the idea, and for a while I obsessed over fully provisioning my workstations and server with Ansible.

items.title: Traefik
items.description: Modern reverse proxy and load balancer. Neat tool that does all the heavy lifting on my home server.

items.title: Azure
items.description: Microsoft's cloud platform. While not an expert by a long stretch, I'm comfortable enough with many of the most common DevOps tasks within Azure.

items.title: Actions
items.description: GitHub's built-in CI/CD automation. Pretty much every single one of my private projects uses GitHub Actions for some combination of integration checks, building, and deploying.

items.title: Pipelines
items.description: Atlassian's Bitbucket CI/CD system. I've maintained my fair share of Bitbucket pipelines in previous positions.
